title: SWTPM_INIT_LOCK mutex with documented scope limitations is the right design
description: device.rs line 9, connection.rs line 14: process-wide mutex that serializes concurrent swtpm init calls. The doc comment explicitly states it does NOT protect against unrelated threads. This is the correct design — promising stronger guarantees would require intercepting all setenv/getenv calls process-wide, which is not feasible. The honest scoped guarantee plus documentation is better than a false safety promise.
